NTC OF ERRATA TO DEFT'S MTN FOR AN ORDER DECLARING DEFT THE PREVAILING PARTY AND AWARDING REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES TO DEFT FILED BY DEFENDANT UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES' MOTION FOR AN ORDER DECLARING PLAINTIFF THE PREVAILING PARTY AND AWARDING REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES TO PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. "WHERE AN ACTION HAS BEEN VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED OR DISMISSED PURSUANT TO A SETTLEMENT OF THE CASE, THERE SHALL BE NO PREVAILING PARTY FOR PURPOSES OF THIS SECTION." (CIVIL CODE 1717(B)(2)) THE FOREGOING IS ILLUSTRATIVE OF CALIFORNIA PUBLIC POLICY AGAINST AN AWARD OF FEES WHERE THE PLAINTIFF VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED THE ACTION, AT LEAST WHERE, AS HERE, NO DISPOSITIVE MOTION WAS PENDING. THE STATUTES CITED BY DEFENDANT IN ITS MOTION ARE NOT ADVANCED BY AN AWARD OF FEES TO A DEFENDANT WHERE THE PLAINTIFF VOLUNTARILY DISMISSED THE ACTION, ESPECIALLY WHEN THE PLAINTIFF IS PRO PER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-29-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-06-2012 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Declarating Pltf The Prevailing Party
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John P. Zanghi, Esq., of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky LLP, Tel. 415.977.0444 x22, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
No other appearance.

RULING: THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT UNITED COUNCIL OF HUMAN SERVICES' Motion For An Order Declaring Plaintiff The Prevailing Party And Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees To Plaintiff is Denied. "Where an action has been voluntarily dismissed or dismissed pursuant to a settlement of the case, there shall be no prevailing party for purposes of this section." (Civil Code 1717(b)(2)) The foregoing is illustrative of California public policy against an award of fees where the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed the action, at least where, as here, no dispositive motion was pending. The statutes cited by defendant in its motion are not advanced by an award of fees to a defendant where the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed the action, especially when the plaintiff is pro per. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

MEDIATION ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
